name: The specialities of the Prophet ﷺ, part 1
epithet: What Allah gave him and no one else
title: The specialities of the Prophet ﷺ, part 1: what Allah gave him alone
meta_description: Day 1 of the seerah: the unique specialities of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, his names, the praised station, and the gifts given to him alone, retold faithfully from Dr. Yasir Qadhi's landmark series.
keywords: seerah, Prophet Muhammad, khasais, specialities of the Prophet, names of the Prophet, maqam mahmud, al-Kawthar, al-wasilah, Yasir Qadhi seerah, life of the Prophet

video.title: Seerah of Prophet Muhammed 1 - Specialities of Prophet Muhammed - Yasir Qadhi | April 2011

source_note: Retold faithfully from Dr. Yasir Qadhi's Seerah of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, episode 1: the specialities of the Prophet ﷺ (Memphis Islamic Center, 2011). Qur'an: Sahih International, verified via quran.ai. The narration is the Sheikh's, the phrasing is Buruja's.
lede: This is day one of a long walk, 104 days through the life of the single greatest human being who ever lived. And before the story begins, before the lineage and the birth and the cave, Dr. Yasir Qadhi opens his landmark seerah series with a question that deserves a whole evening: who is he ﷺ, exactly? What did Allah give this one man that He gave no one else?
The scholars call these the khasais, the specialities. Today is a first handful of them, and by the end of it you will understand why a hundred more days with him ﷺ will not feel like enough.

ayah.text: And raised high for you your repute.

paras: Where do you even begin describing the one Allah chose above all creation? Begin where Allah began: He raised the Prophet's ﷺ remembrance Himself. Ibn Abbas and the early scholars read this ayah and noticed something you can test today: wherever Allah is mentioned, His Messenger ﷺ is mentioned right beside Him. In the shahada. In the adhan. In every tashahhud of every prayer on every continent.

Sheikh Yasir asks you to actually think about this. Not one second passes on this earth, not one, without hundreds of millions of people praising this one man ﷺ: someone is calling the adhan, someone is sitting in prayer, someone is sending salawat. No human being in history has been praised like this, is being praised like this, or will ever be praised like this. That is not fame. That is Allah keeping a promise.

ayah.text: And We have not sent you, [O Muḥammad], except as a mercy to the worlds.

paras: Allah did not say He sent the Prophet ﷺ with mercy, as if mercy were luggage. He said the sending itself is mercy. So he ﷺ is mercy, his message is mercy, his teachings are mercy, and believing in what he brought is mercy reaching you personally, fourteen centuries downstream. Everything associated with him carries the same watermark.
Hold that thought through the next 104 days. Every story you will hear, the persecution, the battles, the losses, the victories, sits inside this one sentence: a mercy to the worlds.

ayah.arabic: وَإِذْ قَالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ يَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَيْكُم مُّصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَمُبَشِّرًا بِرَسُولٍ يَأْتِي مِن بَعْدِي اسْمُهُ أَحْمَدُ ۖ فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ قَالُوا هَٰذَا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
ayah.text: And [mention] when Jesus, the son of Mary, said, "O Children of Israel, indeed I am the messenger of Allāh to you confirming what came before me of the Torah and bringing good tidings of a messenger to come after me, whose name is Aḥmad." But when he came to them with clear evidences, they said, "This is obvious magic."

paras: People gave him many names over the centuries (one early scholar gathered more than 250), but the names that matter most are the ones Allah gave. In a hadith the Prophet ﷺ counted some of them himself: I am Muhammad, and I am Ahmad. I am al-Mahi, the one through whom Allah erases disbelief. I am al-Hashir, at whose feet mankind will be raised. I am al-Aqib, the one after whom there is no prophet. And he called himself the prophet of repentance: through following him, people find forgiveness.
Of all these, the Qur'an itself uses two proper names. Muhammad, four times. And Ahmad, placed in the mouth of Isa, announcing him to the Children of Israel before he ﷺ was ever born.

Both names grow from the same root: hamd, praise. And not casual praise. Arabic separates shukr, the thank-you you give someone for a favor, from hamd, the praise a being deserves simply for what he is. Shukr is a transaction. Hamd is awe. Muhammad means the one praised again and again and again, praise in quantity, praise that never stops. Ahmad means the one praised with the finest praise there is, praise in quality. One of the classical scholars saw a wisdom in which nation heard which name: Musa's people, the largest believing nation before ours, were told of Muhammad, the abundantly praised. Isa's true followers, few, hunted, and utterly devoted, were told of Ahmad, the finest of praise. Quantity for the many, quality for the few, and both names belong to him ﷺ.

ayah.arabic: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ نَافِلَةً لَّكَ عَسَىٰ أَن يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقَامًا مَّحْمُودًا
ayah.text: And from [part of] the night, pray with it [i.e., recitation of the Qur’ān] as additional [worship] for you; it is expected that your Lord will resurrect you to a praised station.

paras: Now the scene the Sheikh builds slowly, because it deserves it. The Day of Judgment. Fifty thousand years long, and the waiting itself is a punishment. Humanity, believer and denier alike, is desperate for the Reckoning to simply begin, and so they go looking for someone to plead with Allah on their behalf.

They go to Adam: you are our father, Allah created you with His own hands. Adam remembers his slip and says, myself, myself, go to another. They go to Nuh, and he remembers the plea he made for his drowning son. To Ibrahim, who remembers three statements that were not even truly lies, and still trembles. To Musa, who remembers the man he struck. To Isa, who simply says: go to another. Every one of them, the greatest souls ever created, says some version of the same thing: not me.

Then all of humanity comes to Muhammad ﷺ. And he does not list his fears, though he has more right than anyone to be careful with Allah. He says: this is mine. I am the one for it. He goes, he falls in prostration, and he is given what no one else is given: permission to intercede for the Judgment to begin. On that day every human being who ever lived will praise him, including those who mocked him, cursed him, and drew their insults of him in this world. They will praise him because they will owe him. That is al-maqam al-mahmud, the praised station this ayah promises, and the name Muhammad will be paid in full in front of everyone.

ayah.arabic: مَّا كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبَا أَحَدٍ مِّن رِّجَالِكُمْ وَلَٰكِن رَّسُولَ اللَّهِ وَخَاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمًا
ayah.text: Muḥammad is not the father of [any] one of your men, but [he is] the Messenger of Allāh and seal [i.e., last] of the prophets. And ever is Allāh, of all things, Knowing.

paras: Then come the specialities themselves, the things on no other prophet's list. He is the final prophet: the long chain of revelation, thousands of years of messengers, ends deliberately at him ﷺ. And his prophethood was no late decision. When a companion asked when he was decreed a prophet, he answered: while Adam was still between spirit and clay. Before the first man breathed, the last messenger was already written.

He is the only prophet sent to all of humanity. Every messenger before him was sent to his own nation (Adam and Nuh only appear universal because, in their day, one family or one township was all the humanity there was). He ﷺ was sent, on purpose, to every nation at once, and further still: he was sent to the jinn. There is a night in Makkah the series will reach, the night of the jinn, when they came and asked him to teach them.
And Allah aided him with something no army has: awe. By his own words ﷺ, Allah cast fear into his enemies at the distance of a month's journey. Empires felt him coming before any rider arrived.

He ﷺ was shown the nations in a vision: he saw a great multitude stretching as far as the eye could see and wondered if it was his ummah. He was told: that is Musa and his people. Then he saw a multitude that swallowed the horizon itself, and was told: this is your ummah.

Another day he asked his companions: would it please you to be a third of the people of Jannah? They cried Allahu Akbar. Half? Allahu Akbar. Then he said: by Allah, I hope that you will be two thirds of the people of Jannah.

Sheikh Yasir stops you here to do the math. When the Prophet ﷺ said this, the believers on earth were perhaps fifteen hundred souls, one small city, surrounded by empires of millions. Today his followers are counted in the billions, and they have never stopped growing for fourteen centuries. When he said it, it was a miracle. We are simply alive late enough to watch it come true with our own eyes.

And the miracle he carries outlives every other kind. The splitting of the sea was witnessed by one generation; you and I were not on the shore. But the Qur'an is the one miracle a Muslim can physically hand to a doubter and say: here, hear it for yourself. It is a living miracle, open access, renewed every time it is recited. No prophet was given anything like it.

ayah.text: Indeed, We have granted you, [O Muḥammad], al-Kawthar.

paras: Only one human being was ever summoned up through the heavens to the presence of Allah. On that journey there came a boundary where even Jibril, the mightiest of angels, stopped and said: my permission ends here, you go on alone. And he ﷺ went on alone. Musa was spoken to on a mountain; Muhammad ﷺ was called up.

He is, by his own report, the sayyid of the children of Adam: the master and leader of humanity, its finest and its representative. On the Day of Judgment the order of everything bows to him: his grave is the first to open, he is the first to be clothed, the first to cross the bridge, the first to knock on the gate of Jannah. The keeper of the gate will ask who is there, and at his name ﷺ will answer: I was commanded to open for no one before you.

His ummah, the last nation in time, will be the first nation through the door, not because of anything in us, but because we are his. And waiting there is his pool, the hawd, so vast that a single side of it runs like half the Arabian Peninsula, and his river, al-Kawthar, the great source from which the rivers of Jannah branch. Drink anywhere in Jannah and you are drinking from a gift Allah gave to him ﷺ.

Jannah narrows as it rises, the Sheikh explains: picture levels climbing toward the Throne, fewer souls at every height. At the very top, directly beneath the Throne of the Most Merciful, there is an entire level of Jannah prepared for exactly one servant. It is called al-wasilah.

The Prophet ﷺ told his companions about it, and then added, with a modesty that should break your heart: I hope that I am that servant. There was never anyone else it could be. But he would not claim it; he asked instead, and he asked us to ask for him.

That is why, fourteen centuries later, every time the adhan finishes, the ummah he loved lifts its hands and asks Allah to grant Muhammad ﷺ al-wasilah. Hundreds of millions of voices, five times a day, praying not for themselves but for him. Day one of the seerah, and you already have your first assignment.

dua.translit: Allahumma salli wa sallim wa barik ala nabiyyina Muhammad
dua.en: O Allah, send Your praise, Your peace, and Your blessings upon our Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

lessons_intro: Before the story even begins, the specialities hand you a way to live. These threads run straight out of the Sheikh's opening night.

lessons.text: Not one second passes without millions praising him ﷺ. When you send salawat, you are not starting something, you are joining something that has never once stopped.
The Sheikh opens with the specialities for a reason: the more you know of what Allah gave him ﷺ, the hungrier you become for his story. Study from love, and the studying carries itself.
You were not at the sea when it split, but the Qur'an sits in your home. The one miracle every generation can hear with its own ears deserves more than a shelf.

lessons.bold: Answer the adhan with him in mind.
lessons.text: The du'a after the adhan asks al-wasilah for him ﷺ. He asked us to pray it for him. Five small moments a day, make them his.
Last in time, first through the gate, and not because of us. Belonging to him ﷺ is the unearned gift; living like we belong to him is the thanks.

faq.q: What are the khasais of the Prophet ﷺ?
faq.a: Khasais are the unique specialities Allah granted the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and no other human being. The early scholars wrote whole books gathering them, and some have counted fifty or more: among them his names, the praised station of intercession, being sent to all of humanity and the jinn, the Qur'an as a living miracle, the hawd, al-Kawthar, and al-wasilah.

faq.q: What is the difference between the names Muhammad and Ahmad?
faq.a: Both come from hamd, the highest form of praise: praise deserved for what someone is, not traded for a favor. Muhammad means the one praised continuously, again and again (praise in quantity). Ahmad means the one praised with the finest praise (praise in quality). Both meanings are fulfilled in him ﷺ.

faq.q: What is al-maqam al-mahmud, the praised station?
faq.a: It is the station promised in Surah al-Isra 17:79. On the Day of Judgment, when every other prophet excuses himself, the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ will intercede before Allah for the Judgment to begin, and all of humanity, even those who rejected him, will praise him for it.

faq.q: Was the Prophet ﷺ really sent to the jinn as well?
faq.a: Yes. Among his specialities is that his message reached both humanity and the jinn. The series later tells of the night of the jinn in Makkah, when a party of jinn came to him ﷺ and asked to be taught Islam.

faq.q: What is al-wasilah, and why do we pray for it after every adhan?
faq.a: Al-wasilah is the highest level of Jannah, an entire level beneath the Throne prepared for a single servant of Allah. The Prophet ﷺ said he hoped to be that servant and asked his ummah to pray it for him, which is why the du'a after every adhan asks Allah to grant him al-wasilah.

closing_heading: Why this day stays with you
closing: One hundred and four days from now you will have walked with him ﷺ from before his birth to beyond his passing. Day one exists so that you walk it the right way: not as history, but as an introduction to someone. The man whose story you are about to hear is the most praised soul in creation, mercy sent walking, the first of the first in the next world, and he is also, as the coming days will show, the most human of teachers: a man who buried children, bled in the street of Ta'if, and still called himself simply a servant.

So begin the way his ummah has always begun, with his name in your mouth. O Allah, send Your praise and Your peace upon Muhammad ﷺ, raise him to al-wasilah and the praised station You promised him, gather us under his banner on the Day the nations are gathered, and let us drink from his hand a drink after which there is no thirst. Ameen.

slides:
Day 1 of 104.

Before the story of his life begins, one night on a single question: who is he ﷺ?

Allah says: We raised high for you your repute.

Wherever Allah is named, His Messenger ﷺ is named beside Him. The shahada. The adhan. Every prayer on earth.

Not one second passes without millions praising him.

Two names in the Qur'an, both from praise.

Muhammad: praised again and again, without end.
Ahmad: praised with the finest praise there is.

Quantity and quality, gathered in one man ﷺ.

The Day of Judgment. Adam says: go to another. Nuh: go to another. Ibrahim, Musa, Isa: go to another.

He ﷺ says: this is mine.

And all creation will praise him. The praised station.

Decreed a prophet while Adam was between spirit and clay.

Sent to every nation, and to the jinn.

The final prophet. No one after him ﷺ.

He was shown his ummah in a vision: a multitude that swallowed the horizon.

He hoped we would be two thirds of the people of Jannah.

He said it when the believers numbered 1,500. Look around now.

First grave to open. First to be clothed. First across the bridge. First knock on the gate of Jannah.

The gatekeeper: I was commanded to open for no one before you ﷺ.

And beneath the Throne, one level of Jannah for a single servant: al-wasilah.

He said, modestly: I hope I am that servant.

After every adhan, ask it for him ﷺ. That is day one's assignment.
social.caption: Day 1 of 104: the seerah of the Prophet ﷺ begins. Before the birth, the lineage, the cave: who is he? The specialities Allah gave him and no one else. Retold faithfully from Dr. Yasir Qadhi's landmark seerah series (episode embedded on the site), Qur'an verified via quran.ai. A new day of his life ﷺ, every day, at buruja.com. #seerah #ProphetMuhammad #Buruja #YasirQadhi #islam #sunnah #salawat

email.subject: Who is he ﷺ, before the story begins
email.alt_subject: Not one second passes without him being praised
email.preview: Day 1 of 104: the specialities Allah gave His Messenger and no one else.
email.body:
Assalamu alaykum,

Today Buruja begins its longest journey yet: 104 days with the life of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, retold faithfully from Dr. Yasir Qadhi's landmark seerah series.

And it does not begin with his birth. It begins with a question: who is he ﷺ?

Allah raised his remembrance so high that wherever Allah is named, he is named beside Him. In the shahada, in the adhan, in every prayer on every continent. Stop and feel this: not one second passes on this earth without hundreds of millions of people praising this one man ﷺ.

Today's entry walks through the specialities Allah gave him alone. His names, Muhammad and Ahmad, praise in quantity and praise in quality. The praised station on the Day of Judgment, when every prophet steps back and he ﷺ steps forward. The pool, the river, the gate of Jannah that opens for no one before him. And al-wasilah, the one level of Jannah meant for a single servant, which he, with heartbreaking modesty, only said he hoped to be given.

He asked us to pray it for him. That is your first assignment of the seerah: after the adhan today, ask al-wasilah for him ﷺ.

Read day 1, and watch the Sheikh's full opening episode, at the link below.
